candidate.objective: Experienced Project Supervisor and Civil Superintendent with over 10 years across the mining, rail, and civil construction industries. Demonstrates strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills with a commitment to quality assurance, health, safety, and environmental leadership and compliance. Proven ability to lead and manage multi-disciplinary work groups across multiple project fronts, liaise effectively with clients, and deliver projects on time and within budget in challenging operational environments.

duties: Supervising civil construction activities at the Eliwana Mine for Fortescue Metals Group (FMG), including haul road diversions, pipe burials, transfer ponds and turkey nest construction, land clearing and grubbing, and construction of access pads.
Ensuring all works are delivered in accordance with client specifications, safety requirements, and project milestones.
Managing and directing site crews across multiple concurrent work fronts.
Conducting daily pre-start meetings and enforcing site safety and compliance standards.

employment.employer: Laing O'Rourke — Parramatta Rail Stabling and Maintenance Facility
duties: Supervising civil construction works at the Parramatta Rail Stabling and Maintenance Facility.
Managing site crews and coordinating civil activities to meet program milestones.
Ensuring compliance with project safety, quality, and schedule requirements.
Supervising Rio Tinto rail renewal works in the Pilbara, including removal and replacement of rail, ballast, and formation.
Managing all construction activities during operational rail shutdowns to strict time constraints, with significant financial penalties applicable for any time overruns.
Monitoring all construction activities for safety compliance, schedule adherence, quality, and continuity of works.
Successfully completed all rail shutdowns ahead of schedule, handing back rail assets to the client ahead of time and ensuring full client satisfaction with respect to quality and safety.
Overseeing a bushfire recovery project, formulating scope of work for managed properties, and managing and allocating contractors across the recovery program.
Developing and monitoring construction schedules, overseeing quality control, and assessing contractor performance.
Ensuring the correct methodology and legal compliance in the removal and dismantling of hazardous and toxic materials, including asbestos, in accordance with Local, State, and Federal legislation.
Ensuring correct personal protective equipment (PPE) was available and accessible and that all WHS requirements were implemented across all recovery activities.
Preparing complex documentation and liaising with senior management to provide project progress updates.
Implementing troubleshooting and problem-solving skills to meet project budget and timeframe requirements.
Completed the project on time and within budget with no major incidents.
Planning, implementing, and managing construction tasks across large-scale civil infrastructure projects in accordance with business priorities and goals.
Managing project and employee schedules, monitoring attendance, and maintaining quality of works across multiple project fronts.
Adhering to and enforcing relevant safety regulations and guiding the use of machinery and equipment.
Managing expenditure and ensuring compliance with allocated budget.
Resolving emerging problems and issues as they arose, delegating responsibilities, and supervising, training, and providing constructive feedback to workers.

Overseeing construction of an operations control centre, earthworks, pedestrian bridge, rail station stops, high-voltage sub-station, utilities networks, drainage, pavement, concreting, and screening, along with demolition of existing structures.
Issuing permits for all site activities, scheduling contractors, and maintaining timeframe and budget control throughout delivery.
Completed the project on time and within budget, demonstrating developed industry networks and a strong capability for quality control.
Overseeing the nightshift refurbishment of the operational Wynyard Station while it remained open to the general public during construction works.
Developing, implementing, and monitoring site safety, including availability and correct use of PPE.
Coordinating construction activities across departments including the engineering sector to ensure efficient and timely delivery above client expectations.
Conducting risk and quality control assessments, delegating and negotiating daily tasks and duties, and completing all required permits for each shift.
Motivated and inspired a positive team environment and collaborative working culture.
Overseeing Kellogg Brown Root Infrastructure building, earthworks, and civil operations at the Ichthys Gas Project.
Developing and managing production schedules to meet internal volume, cost, and quality targets.
Analysing production metrics and identifying solutions to operational issues as they arose.
Managing staffing and contractors while maintaining a safe and compliant work environment.
Overseeing construction of site facilities for the operations centre and coordinating maintenance of all construction facilities during commissioning.
Assisting with the coordination of earthworks and pavement for haulage roads, civil works and construction.
Conducting drainage and piling works, scheduling construction activities, and maintaining awareness of timeframe and budget controls.
Obtaining and signing off on permits, coordinating and delegating daily tasks and goals for trades, contractors, and workers.
Monitoring performance and providing constructive feedback to develop workers' skills and capabilities.

skills.detail: Over 10 years of experience supervising complex civil, mining, rail, and pipeline construction projects across Australia, with demonstrated capability in managing multi-trade crews, scheduling, budget control, and client liaison.

Holds Diploma of Occupational Health and Safety and Certificate IV in OHS. Experienced in developing and enforcing WHS management plans, issuing work permits, conducting hazard analyses, and managing asbestos removal in compliance with relevant legislation.
Holds Certificate IV in Training and Assessment (TAE40110). Experienced in mentoring, coaching, and conducting performance assessments for site personnel.
Experienced in rail renewal, rail line shutdown management, and construction activities in live rail environments, including safe access to rail corridors.

certifications.details: Appointed Person under Section 44 for emergency fire management.
Certified to issue work permits for hazardous and high-risk activities.

certifications.details: Certified to work in accordance with issued work permits.

certifications.details: Certified in the use of first attack firefighting equipment.
Certified to conduct formal hazard analyses.

certifications.ticket: TLIF2080 — Safely Access the Rail Corridor
certifications.details: Certified for safe access to live rail corridors.

Certificate I in Workplace Environmental Responsibilities
certifications.details: Certificate I in Workplace Environmental Responsibilities (30982).

certifications.details: Certificate IV in Training and Assessment — qualified to deliver and assess training.
